summary: Accepted for extraction_fidelity. The proposed coverage assessment keeps DKD, IPF, and AD-risk claims at metadata-only abstract extraction, preserves source-located D+Q bone registry and full-text safety extraction, and leaves full article/table and supplement extraction debt explicit.

findings.claim_or_issue: The coverage assessment describes DKD, IPF, and AD-risk PubMed EFetch artifacts as metadata-only abstract-level support for sample, dosing, timing, effect, and safety details.
findings.why_it_matters: This matches the bounded source support and prevents abstract snapshots from being treated as full-text or table extraction.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required for this lane; keep the abstract-only boundary in downstream synthesis readiness checks.

findings.claim_or_issue: The D+Q bone safety update is limited to registry aggregates, retained full-text adverse-event table rows, and registry/article discontinuation fields.
findings.why_it_matters: The proposed assessment ties extracted safety details to retained registry and PMC article locations instead of inventing unsupported harmonized safety outcomes.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required for this lane; full-text/table supplement extraction remains a separate open gap.

findings.claim_or_issue: Known gaps and next actions state that full article/table extraction remains incomplete for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q supplement material.
findings.why_it_matters: The candidate does not upgrade triage or abstract-backed material into synthesis-ready exact-effect evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required for this lane; retain the extraction debt until full-text or table-row snapshots are ingested and reconciled.
